public_links.title: Wikipedia: Bio-sand Filter Mechanical & Biological Water Treatment Principles
CAWST: Open-Source Bio-sand Filter Technical Project Design Manual

technical_documents.description: An off-grid, low-cost decentralized water purification infrastructure blueprint utilizing layered geologic media and a self-sustaining biological community. By combining mechanical depth filtration with active microbial predation, this gravity-driven slow-sand system eliminates waterborne pathogens and turbidity from contaminated surface water sources without chemical additives or active power consumption.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Over 783 million people globally continue to experience severe water insecurity due to a total lack of clean drinking water infrastructure. While commercial point-of-use ceramic filters offer localized purification, their high unit costs ($25+ USD) and rapid mechanical clogging issues render them unsustainable in off-grid remote sectors where replacement parts and specialized maintenance protocols are physically unavailable.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The design retrofits a food-grade 200L steel containment vessel into a high-surface-area slow-sand filtration column. Raw influent is introduced over a physical diffuser plate to protect the underlying media bed, passing down through a 50cm column of sieved fine silica sand ($0.15	ext{--}0.35	ext{mm}$ grain size) supported by a graded medium-to-coarse gravel under-drainage matrix. Pathogen destruction relies on a two-pronged mechanism: immediate mechanical depth-straining paired with bio-filtration via a top-tier active biological layer (schmutzdecke) that matures within a 14-day initialization phase. This self-regulating microbial ecosystem neutralizes >99% of viable E. coli colonies and suspended solids, delivering a regulated, gravity-driven safe yield of 0.4 L/min.

bill_of_materials.name: Food-Grade Anti-Corrosive Cylindrical Steel Drum (200L Capacity)
Sieved High-Silica Fine Filtration Sand 0.15-0.35mm (60 kg)
Washed and Graded Capillary Support Gravel 6-15mm (20 kg)
Heavy-Duty PVC Outlet Tap Valve & Perforated Internal Diffuser Plate
Fine-Mesh Synthetic Screen Layer (Geotextile Media Separator)

specifications.filtration_rate: 0.4 L/min via slow-sand gravity-fed fluid mechanics

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Cut the drum top off; drill a 20mm hole 5cm from the bottom for the outlet tap.
Step 2: Layer 10cm of coarse gravel at the base, then 5cm of medium gravel; cover with mesh separator.
Step 3: Add 50cm of sieved fine sand (0.15–0.35mm grain size), levelling the surface carefully.
Step 4: Place a diffuser plate (drilled plastic lid) on the sand surface to prevent disturbance when pouring.
Step 5: Fill with source water and let sit for 2 weeks without use to develop the biological schmutzdecke layer; then filter at 0.4L/min maximum.

comments.text: Tested output at a lab — 99.4% E. coli removal. Our village of 200 people runs 4 of these.
